summary: Portable bStocks strategy lessons for the BNB hackathon lane. These lessons review strategy specs and activation quality, not live execution.

lessons.summary: Never approve a bStocks setup without matching issuer, venue, symbol, and CMC identity.
lessons.effect: Reject or hold the strategy inactive until lane identity is explicit.

lessons.summary: Broad CMC regime gates must stay visible before any bStocks relative-strength logic is allowed to activate.
lessons.effect: Keep the strategy inactive when regime gates fail instead of rescuing it with single-name momentum.

lessons.summary: Do not activate a rotation lane unless the tracked bStocks universe has enough quoteable names to rank.
lessons.effect: Wait or reject until the minimum universe coverage threshold is restored.

lessons.summary: Thin-volume names should not dominate the strategy thesis even if they are top 24h performers.
lessons.effect: Prefer the more active names and keep concentration controls explicit.

lessons.summary: Missing non-CMC external sentiment or narrative data is neutral, not bullish or bearish.
lessons.effect: Do not invent external evidence; use only labeled CMC-backed or repo-maintained inputs.

lessons.summary: Mixed regimes can still justify a strategy spec, but only with reduced sizing, exposure caps, and explicit invalidation.
lessons.effect: Keep the strategy narrow and preserve risk controls instead of broad deployment.
